other_observers: Tony Leukering; I believe the bird was first seen in April by David Leatherman.
description: A relatively large woodpecker and typical in shape and profile of other Melanerpes. The bill as rather long, black, and chisel-shaped. The nasal area was composed of puffy orange feathers contrasting with a gray crown. The rear crown and nape were red. The rest of the face, throat, breast, belly, and flanks were buffy-white. The mantle and wings were black basally with extensive white bars laterally defined across the entire region. The buffy-white undertail coverts were scaled black. The uppertail coverts were white and the tail was mostly balck, except for interrupted white along the central and outer rectrices.  I was able to obtain poor photographs of the bird, but that are diagnostic for the species.Behaviors: Perched in Siberian Elm. Occasionally chiseled at bark.Call: Gave a sharp, loud kwirrr call.Plumage: alternate
